name: carnauba wax
description: Carnauba wax is a plant-derived food-grade wax used as a stabilizer and glazing agent to provide protective coatings and structural integrity to edible products.
raw_text: Copernicia Cerifera Wax (Explained + Products) Products Ingredients Decode INCI Login Register Login Register Products Ingredients Decode INCI Follow us on our new Insta page » Copernicia Cerifera Wax Also-called-like-this: Carnauba Wax What-it-does: emollient Irritancy: 0 Comedogenicity: 1 Official CosIng Information All Functions: depilatory Description: copernicia cerifera (carnauba) wax is a wax obtained from the leaves and leaf buds of copernicia cerifera, palmaceae. CAS #: 8015-86-9 | EC #: 232-399-4 Details A vegetable wax coming from the leaves of the Brazilian tropical palm tree, Copernicia cerifera. Similar to other waxes, it is used to stabilize and give body to products , or to keep stick type formulas solid. It is the hardest natural wax with a high melting point (around 85C) and high gloss making it a great wax choice for lip products. [more] [more] [less] Something incorrect or missing? Let us know!
